title: Immunofluorescent Staining of Mouse Pancreas for Islet Cell Mass Analysis
author: Islet and Pancreas Analysis Core
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bzvwp67e

steps.title: Gather reagents for immunostaining, noting that...
steps.instruction: Gather reagents for immunostaining, noting that steps 9-11, 15, and 17-18 can be performed in Kartell staining chambers (each holds ~50 mL). Always use freshly-made antibody and wash buffers (steps 4, 6); other reagents can be prepared ahead of time and stored at 4 °C .

steps.title: Acquire images of stained pancreas sections...
steps.instruction: Acquire images of stained pancreas sections using a high-resolution whole slide scanning system (Aperio ScanScope FL, Leica Biosystems). An example field of view is shown below in Figure 1 .

steps.title: Let the frozen cryosections thaw at...
steps.instruction: Let the frozen cryosections thaw at room temperature and air-dry for about 00:30:00 .

steps.title: To remove OCT, wash slides 3...
steps.instruction: To remove OCT, wash slides 3 times in 1X PBS for 00:05:00 , decanting or switching to a new chamber after each wash.

steps.title: Permeabilize the tissue section with permeabilization...
steps.instruction: Permeabilize the tissue section with permeabilization solution for 00:15:00 at room temperature.

steps.title: Wash the sections 3 times in...
steps.instruction: Wash the sections 3 times in 1X PBS for 00:05:00 , decanting or switching to a new chamber after each wash.

steps.title: Draw rectangles around the sections with...
steps.instruction: Draw rectangles around the sections with PAP pen and let them dry for about 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Block the sections with blocking buffer...
steps.instruction: Block the sections with blocking buffer at room temperature for 01:30:00 in a humidified chamber. Use a sufficient volume (approximately 200-300 µl) to ensure each section is entirely covered with buffer.

steps.title: Aspirate the blocking buffer, add primary...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the blocking buffer, add primary antibodies diluted in antibody buffer (see Table 1 ), and incubate in a humidified chamber Overnight at 4 °C . Use a sufficient volume (approximately 200-300 µl) to ensure each section is entirely covered with antibody solution. Table 1: Detailed information for primary and secondary antibodies.

steps.title: Aspirate the primary antibody solution and...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the primary antibody solution and wash the sections 3 times in wash buffer for 00:10:00 , decanting or switching to a new chamber after each wash.

steps.title: Add secondary antibodies diluted in antibody...
steps.instruction: Add secondary antibodies diluted in antibody buffer (see Table 1 ) and incubate for 01:30:00 at room temperature in a humidified chamber. Use a sufficient volume (approximately 200-300 µl) to ensure each section is entirely covered with antibody solution.

steps.title: Aspirate the secondary antibody solution and...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the secondary antibody solution and incubate slides in DAPI solution to counterstain for 00:10:00 at room temperature.

steps.title: Wash slides 3 times in 1x...
steps.instruction: Wash slides 3 times in 1x PBS for 00:15:00 , decanting or switching to a new chamber after each wash.

steps.title: Add a sufficient volume of SlowFade...
steps.instruction: Add a sufficient volume of SlowFade Gold mounting medium (approximately 5-10 µl) to each tissue section, ensuring tissue section will be entirely covered after medium spreads. Carefully mount coverslips, making sure to avoid creating air bubbles between the tissue and coverslip.

steps.title: Blot away any excess mounting medium...
steps.instruction: Blot away any excess mounting medium from slide edges and seal the coverslipped slides with nail polish on all edges. Allow slides to dry completely before imaging.

steps.title: Apply a tissue classifier algorithm (HALO®...
steps.instruction: Apply a tissue classifier algorithm (HALO® image analysis platform, Indica Labs) to quantify cross-sectional area of islet alpha (glucagon+), beta (insulin+), and delta (somatostatin+) cells. An example markup image is shown in Figure 2 . Figure 1 (above left). Fluorescent imaging of mouse pancreatic tissue. Figure 2 (above right). Analysis of mouse pancreatic islets using a tissue classifier algorithm. To quantify islet cell mass for a given mouse, stain and analyze at least 5 cryosections spaced approximately 200 μm apart . Each islet cell type is reported as a percentage of total alpha, beta, and delta cell area. Islet cell mass is calculated by multiplying alpha, beta, and/or delta cell area by pancreas weight.

steps.title: 10% Triton X-100 stock (30 mL)...
steps.instruction: 10% Triton X-100 stock (30 mL) – Combine 3 mL Triton-X-100 and 27 mL 1X PBS. Mix on shaker for 00:30:00 or until Triton X-100 is completely dissolved. Store at 4 °C for up to 1 month.

steps.title: Permeabilization solution (0
steps.instruction: Permeabilization solution (0.2% Triton, 50 mL) – Combine 1 mL 10% Triton stock and 49 mL 1X PBS.

steps.title: Blocking buffer (5% Normal Donkey Serum,...
steps.instruction: Blocking buffer (5% Normal Donkey Serum, 4 mL) – Combine 0.2 mL NDS and 3.8 mL 1X PBS.

steps.title: Antibody buffer (10 mL) – Combine 0
steps.instruction: Antibody buffer (10 mL) – Combine 0.1 g BSA, 0.1 mL 10% Triton stock, and 9.8 mL 1X PBS. Filter solution through a 0.22 μm syringe filter and store at 4 °C .

steps.title: DAPI staining solution (1:25,000, 50 mL)...
steps.instruction: DAPI staining solution (1:25,000, 50 mL) – Combine 2 µl DAPI stock (5mg/mL) and 50 mL 1X PBS. Keep protected from light or prepare right before step 17.

steps.title: Wash buffer (150 mL) - Combine 148
steps.instruction: Wash buffer (150 mL) - Combine 148.5 mL 1X PBS and 1.5 mL 10% Triton X-100 stock.
